title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Revolution's pressing led to Langoni's decisive goal in the second half after forcing a Minnesota mistake.
Goalkeeper Matt Turner made crucial saves for New England, responding well after a midweek criticism against Nashville.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted 'SNL' season 51 finale on May 16, with Paul McCartney as the musical guest.
Ferrell's cold open featured him as Jeffrey Epstein's ghost visiting James Austin Johnson's Donald Trump.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including 'Days We Left Behind' from his upcoming album, with Chad Smith on drums.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will run from July 11-16, featuring vessels from over 25 countries.
The first Sail Boston event in 1976 coincided with Queen Elizabeth II's visit.
Sail Boston events in the past faced protests, notably in 1992 and 2000, related to historical slave trade concerns.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to lead Patriots rookie minicamp amid energy concerns following offseason rumors.
Vibe at Patriots facilities reportedly improves as Vrabel regains focus before the demanding 2026 season.
New safety Kevin Byard praises Vrabel's consistency and energy, comparing him favorably to his time in Tennessee.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive two-run homer in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 win over the Braves.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings, allowing two runs and four hits, before Aroldis Chapman secured the save.
The victory marked the Red Sox's first comeback win when trailing after seven innings in the 2026 season.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox placed Trevor Story on 10-day injured list with a sports hernia before facing the Atlanta Braves.
Nick Sogard was recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Trevor Story on the roster.
Story has struggled this season, batting .206 with a .547 OPS and committing six errors in 39 games.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: David Peloski, 74, died after a head-on collision with a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
The school bus, driven by James French, carried 13 students; none sustained serious injuries.
New Hampshire State Police are investigating the crash involving a school bus and a pickup truck.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's 10th inning walk-off hit leads Braves past Red Sox 3-2.
Red Sox fall to 18-26 despite Marcelo Mayer's game-tying home run in the seventh inning.
Boston left seven runners on base, continuing struggle with scoring opportunities.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs interested in returning to Patriots, but A.J. Brown trade complicates reunion.
Patriots expected to absorb $32 million AAV from A.J. Brown, limiting cap space for Diggs.
Patriots unlikely to pursue Diggs, who seeks top receiver role after 1,000-yard season.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester, Boston police report.
LaGrant-Gomes is described as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, brown eyes, black hair, known to frequent Columbia Road.
Police urge anyone with information on LaGrant-Gomes to contact District B-3 detectives or submit tips via CrimeStoppers.
